config.title: Tempelhof Airport Berlin - FAQ
config.subtitle: Quick answers about Tempelhofer Feld, airport tours, entrances, opening hours and whether Tempelhof fits your Berlin trip.

items.q: Is Tempelhof Airport Berlin worth visiting?
items.a: Yes, if you want **one of Berlin's most unusual open spaces** or a deeper airport/history layer. For a first visit, start with the free field. Book a building tour only if the airport interior is a real interest.
Yes. The official Tempelhofer Feld visitor page lists **free admission**. The airport building is different: official guided tours are separate and should be checked or booked before you go.

items.q: Which entrance is best for Tempelhofer Feld?
items.a: Use **S+U Tempelhof / Tempelhofer Damm** for the easiest first runway look. Use **Platz der Luftbrücke** for the airport building and Airlift Memorial. Use the **Oderstraße / Neukölln side** for local park life, lawns and food after.

items.q: What are Tempelhofer Feld opening hours?
items.a: The field has **seasonal opening hours**. When this guide was checked, June and July were listed as 6:00 AM to 11:00 PM, August as 6:00 AM to 10:30 PM, and December as 7:30 AM to 5:00 PM. Check the official page before a sunset or late visit.

items.q: How long do I need at Tempelhof?
items.a: Plan **45 minutes** for a quick runway look, **90 minutes** for a comfortable first loop, or **2 to 3 hours** if you add a longer field visit or an official airport tour.

items.q: Can I visit Tempelhof with luggage?
items.a: A small backpack is fine, but a suitcase makes Tempelhof awkward. The field is huge, exposed and not a good luggage walk. Use station lockers or luggage storage first, especially if you are arriving through Hauptbahnhof.
